name: Dwarf Treasure Hunters
source: The New Mordheimer, https://mordheimer.net/docs/warbands/grade-1a-warbands/dwarf-treasure-hunters (fetched 2026-07-06). Grade 1a warband, author Mark Havener. Cited source: Town Cryer #4, revised in the Mordheim Annual 2002. Owner should cross-check against their own copy of that supplement/annual.

heroSlots.notes: 85 gc. Leader: any warrior within 6" may use his Leadership instead of their own. statMaximums null: Dwarfs are not Human — fill from the Dwarf Maximum Profile table if/when sourced.

50 gc, 0-1. Expert Weaponsmith: while he takes part in a battle, missile weapons bought from the warband's basic equipment lists get +3" range for pistols and +6" for crossbows/handguns (applies even if he's not yet entered play or has already been taken Out of Action that battle; no effect on Hired Sword equipment). statMaximums null: fill from the Dwarf Maximum Profile table if/when sourced.

50 gc, 0-2. May be equipped with weapons (not missile weapons) from the Dwarf Warrior equipment list, and may never wear any armour. Deathwish: immune to all psychology, never needs to test for fighting alone. Slayer Skills: may pick from the Troll Slayer skill list as well as their normal lists. statMaximums null: fill from the Dwarf Maximum Profile table if/when sourced.

henchmenTypes.notes: 40 gc, any number. statMaximums null: fill from the Dwarf Maximum Profile table if/when sourced.
40 gc, 0-5. statMaximums null: fill from the Dwarf Maximum Profile table if/when sourced.
25 gc, any number. Young Dwarfs seeking their fortune. statMaximums null: fill from the Dwarf Maximum Profile table if/when sourced.

exclusiveEquipment.name: Dwarf Axe
exclusiveEquipment.restriction: Dwarf warbands only — included in the warband's own recruitment list at listed cost (no roll needed at recruitment); a Rare 8 roll is only needed when buying one later via the Trading Post.
exclusiveEquipment.rulesText: Close combat, Strength as user. Cutting Edge: an extra -1 armour save modifier (e.g. a Strength 4 user gets -2 total). Parry: the wielder may attempt to parry one blow per combat phase — roll a D6 higher than the opponent's highest to-hit roll to negate it (never against an attack from double-or-more the wielder's own Strength). Two Dwarf axes (or a Dwarf axe + another Parry weapon) still only parries once, but may re-roll a failed parry.

specialRules: Dwarf Nobles fallen on hard times — a hold overrun, a disgrace suffered — gather kin and friends to go treasure hunting rather than wallow, and the greatest hoard in the Known World is rumoured to lie in Mordheim. All Dwarfs: Hard to Kill (only taken Out of Action on a roll of 6 on the Injury chart, not 5-6 — treat 1-2 as Knocked Down, 3-5 as Stunned, 6 as Out of Action). Hard Head: ignore special rules for maces, clubs, etc. Armour: never suffer movement penalties for wearing armour. Hate Orcs and Goblins (see Psychology). Grudgebearers: may never include any Elven Hired Sword or Dramatis Personae. Incomparable Miners: +1 to the number of wyrdstone shards found when checking the Exploration chart.
